papers.title_en: DEFENGRAPH: Knowledge Graph-Enhanced LLMs for Blue Team Cyber Defense
papers.authors: Zhen Wang, Kristen Moore, Qin Wang, Guangsheng Yu, Minjune Kim, Diksha Goel, Gang Li, Ahmed Ibrahim, Ahmad Mohsin, Helge Janicke
papers.evidence: 整合知识图谱与大语言模型以增强网络防御推理
papers.tldr: 针对大语言模型在网络安全防御中幻觉严重、时序推理弱、缺乏系统上下文的问题，本文提出DEFENGRAPH框架，通过双层静态-动态知识图谱与图路径检索、LLM上下文过滤及推理重排序增强决策支持。在模拟关键基础设施攻击的红蓝对抗演习数据上，DEFENGRAPH将GPT-4o推理召回从61.45%提升至73.49%，工单-动作召回由52.17%升至72.46%，最多输出50个正确防御动作。该工作为蓝队高动态防御提供了可靠、上下文感知的LLM增强方案。
papers.abstract_en: Large Language Models (LLMs) show promise for supporting decision-making in cybersecurity, but their reliability in high-stakes, time-evolving environments remains limited due to hallucinations, poor temporal reasoning, and shallow grounding in system context. We introduce DEFENGRAPH, an LLM-driven assistant designed to support human defenders during cybersecurity incidents. DEFENGRAPH improves contextual reasoning by integrating a dual-layer Static-Dynamic Knowledge Graph (KG) with graph-based path retrieval, LLM-driven contextual filtering, and reasoning-based re-ranking. The framework grounds LLM outputs in both long-term domain knowledge and evolving event context, enabling faithful and temporally aware decision support. We evaluate DEFENGRAPH in a cyber defense setting using knowledge graphs constructed from heterogeneous security artifacts, including SIEM alerts, system topology, attacker behaviors, and prior defensive actions. The evaluation uses data collected during live Red vs. Blue team cyber range exercises simulating attacks on critical infrastructure, which generate realistic and noisy datasets reflecting real-world defender workflows and system dynamics. Evaluations across four prevalent LLMs show that DEFENGRAPH sets a new state-of-the-art: on GPT-4o it boosts reasoning-recall from 61.45\% to 73.49\% and ticket-action recall from 52.17% to 72.46% (precision 24.49\% to 29.24\%), with similar gains on LLaMA-3 (46.99\% to 61.45\%), DeepSeek-R1 (45.78\% to 56.63\%) and QWen-3 (51.81\% to 59.04\%), while surfacing up to 50 correct defense actions versus 36 for the next best baseline and holding fault rates steady.

papers.title_en: All Relations Lead to Rome: Automated Knowledge Graph Creation and Question Generation
papers.authors: Matthijs Jansen op de Haar, Tobias Stähle, Lorenzo Gatti
papers.evidence: 利用LLM构建知识图谱用于统一推理
papers.tldr: 大语言模型显著提升了信息检索与问答，但现有数据集割裂了向量检索与知识图谱推理，未提供统一表示，且缺乏与语料对齐的事实问答对。为此，我们提出ARLtR框架，自动联合构建知识图谱、嵌入和基于事实的问答对，其问答对明确扎根于提取的实体和支撑证据。我们构建了罗马帝国历史数据集，含1.9万实体、1.6万文本块和8.4千问答对。通过紧密耦合符号图表示与密集检索表示，ARLtR为开发混合检索系统及语义引导方法提供了单一连贯资源。
papers.abstract_en: Large language models have substantially improved information retrieval and question answering; however, existing datasets generally support either vector-based retrieval over unstructured text or reasoning over knowledge graphs, without providing a unified representation that combines both paradigms. Moreover, current benchmarks rarely provide ground-truth entities, relations, and fact-grounded question-answer pairs aligned with the underlying corpus. To address this gap, we introduce All Relations Lead to Rome (ARLtR), a unified framework for automated knowledge graph construction and fact-grounded question-answer generation. ARLtR jointly constructs a knowledge graph, embeddings, and question-answer pairs that are explicitly grounded in extracted entities, relations, and supporting textual evidence. We further instantiate the framework as a historical dataset centered on the Roman Empire, comprising over 19,000 entities, 16,000 chunks, and 8,400 question-answer pairs (https://huggingface.co/datasets/FaynePro/all-relations-lead-to-rome). By tightly coupling symbolic graph representations with dense retrieval representations, ARLtR facilitates the evaluation and development of hybrid retrieval systems and semantic steering approaches within a single coherent resource.
